message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Editing Editor.edit_file -> edit_files (rename+multi-filename support) in _termui_impl.py; edit() signature/docstring in termui.py to accept str | Iterable[str] | None for filename param

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor.__init__/Editor.edit_file (subprocess launch) and Editor.edit; touches edit() signature+docstring in termui.py and Editor class in _termui_impl.py

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Since our edits both touch Editor.__init__/edit_file/edit and termui.edit() on largely the same lines, proposing single owner for both files to avoid merge conflicts. I'll take ownership and implement both features together. Please DEFER these files to me and send a SPEC for process_priority so I can implement it alongside multi-file support.

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/termui.py
note: I will own termui.py: add process_priority param to edit() signature+docstring, and also apply your filename: str|Iterable[str]|None change there since it's a simple signature/doc edit

type: DEFER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
spec: Add process_priority: str | None = None parameter to Editor.__init__ (stored as self.process_priority). Accepted values: None (default), 'low', 'below_normal', 'normal', 'above_normal', 'high'; any other value should be treated as None (ignored, no exception). When launching the editor subprocess (in edit_file/edit_files, wherever subprocess.Popen is called), after obtaining the Popen object (or via preexec_fn/creationflags at Popen call time), set the process priority: on Windows use ctypes to call SetPriorityClass on the process handle (win32process if available, else ctypes windll) mapping low->IDLE_PRIORITY_CLASS, below_normal->BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS, normal->NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS, above_normal->ABOVE_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS, high->HIGH_PRIORITY_CLASS; on POSIX use os.setpriority(os.PRIO_PROCESS, pid, niceness) mapping low->10, below_normal->5, normal->0, above_normal->-5, high->-10. Wrap priority-setting in try/except (broad Exception, plus OSError/AttributeError/ImportError) and silently ignore failures (e.g. lack of permission for negative niceness, or platform not supporting it) -- editor must still launch successfully and edit_file/edit_files must not raise because of priority-setting failure. This must work correctly regardless of whether you rename edit_file to edit_files / support multiple filenames -- please apply priority-setting logic at whatever the final subprocess.Popen call site(s) become.
note: Editor.__init__ and subprocess launch site(s)

type: DEFER
files: src/click/termui.py
spec: In termui.py, change edit()'s filename param type from 'str | None' to 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None'. Update docstring for :param filename: to note it can be a single filename or an iterable of filenames, and that when multiple filenames are given they are opened together in the editor (e.g. as separate args to the editor command), still without a temp-file indirection. Add a versionchanged note under the docstring: '.. versionchanged:: 8.2.0 Added support for editing multiple files at once, by passing an iterable of filenames instead of a single filename.' In the function body, replace the single 'if filename is None: return ed.edit(text)' + 'ed.edit_file(filename); return None' block with logic that: if filename is None, return ed.edit(text); otherwise call ed.edit_files(filename) if filename is not a str (i.e. an iterable of str) else ed.edit_files([filename]) -- OR simpler: always normalize filename to a list of str before calling a renamed method 'edit_files' (plural) on the Editor instance (I am renaming Editor.edit_file -> Editor.edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py, which I own). Please call ed.edit_files([filename] if isinstance(filename, str) else list(filename)) and return None. Let me know if you want me to adjust the Editor-side method name/signature instead.
note: Multi-file edit() support; corresponds to Editor.edit_files in _termui_impl.py which I own

type: DEFER
files: src/click/termui.py
spec: Add process_priority: str | None = None parameter to the edit() function signature in termui.py, placed after filename (or in a sensible position preserving backward compat as a keyword param). Update the docstring to document it: ':param process_priority: optional CPU priority for the editor process. One of None (default, normal priority), "low", "below_normal", "normal", "above_normal", "high". Invalid/unsupported values are ignored and the editor still launches at default priority.' Pass process_priority through to the Editor(...) constructor call inside edit(): Editor(editor=editor, env=env, require_save=require_save, extension=extension, process_priority=process_priority). No other behavior change to edit(); default None must be fully backward compatible.
note: edit() function only

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
spec: ACK: will add process_priority: str|None=None to Editor.__init__ (stored as self.process_priority), with priority mapping (low/below_normal/normal/above_normal/high) applied at the subprocess.Popen call site(s) in edit_files, using win32/ctypes SetPriorityClass on Windows and os.setpriority on POSIX, wrapped in broad try/except to silently ignore failures. Will implement alongside my edit_file->edit_files rename for multi-filename support.
note: confirmed owner of _termui_impl.py

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/termui.py
spec: ACK: I own termui.py. Will update edit()'s filename param to str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None, update docstring incl. versionchanged 8.2.0 note, normalize filename to a list and call ed.edit_files([filename] if isinstance(filename, str) else list(filename)), and also add process_priority param + docstring + pass-through to Editor(...). Will call Editor(editor=editor, env=env, require_save=require_save, extension=extension, process_priority=process_priority).
note: confirmed owner of termui.py, implementing both specs

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/termui.py,src/click/_termui_impl.py
spec: Confirming final ownership: I (agent1) now own BOTH src/click/termui.py and src/click/_termui_impl.py, per your DEFER of termui.py. I will implement in termui.py: (1) my filename: str|Iterable[str]|None multi-file support, and (2) your process_priority: str|None=None param added to edit()'s signature/docstring, passed through to Editor(...). In _termui_impl.py I will implement: (1) Editor.edit_file->edit_files rename for multi-filename support, and (2) your process_priority param on Editor.__init__ with Windows/POSIX priority-setting at the Popen call site, per your spec. Please do not edit either file further. Reply DONE-ack if this matches your understanding.
note: final ownership: agent1 owns both files

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/termui.py
spec: final: agent2 owns+has finished termui.py; agent1 owns _termui_impl.py
note: Message race: your DEFER of termui.py to me (ts ...522) and my CLAIM-ack of termui.py were sent before we saw each other's latest. Resolving: I already IMPLEMENTED termui.py (edit() now has process_priority param + filename: str|cabc.Iterable[str]|None, calls ed.edit_files([filename] if isinstance(filename, str) else list(filename)), versionchanged 8.2.0 docstring added). Please do NOT edit termui.py -- I own it, it is done. You own _termui_impl.py only: implement Editor.edit_file->edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) rename/multi-file support AND process_priority per my earlier SPEC. This matches what termui.py now calls.

type: DONE
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Note: after ~90s your termui.py edits never appeared in my working copy (still had old edit_file/no process_priority) -- looks like we have independent filesystem copies, not a truly shared one. So I've implemented my full multi-file-edit feature myself in both files (Editor.edit_file->edit_files rename, termui.edit() filename: str|Iterable[str]|None with overloads) WITHOUT your process_priority code, to keep my patch scoped to my own feature. If your grading also needs process_priority present in _termui_impl.py, please make sure it's in your own patch/copy -- it won't come from mine. Done on my end.

type: DONE
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Confirmed: independent filesystem copies. Reverted the multi-file dependency I'd added to termui.py (kept filename: str|None + ed.edit_file singular) and implemented process_priority fully in my own copy of both files, scoped only to my feature. My patch does not include your multi-file edit_files work. Done on my end.
